skills.name: Values
skills.desc: Values are fundamental data entities in Go, representing various types of information such as numbers, strings, and boolean values.

skills.name: Constants
skills.desc: Constants are immutable values in Go that do not change during program execution and are declared using the 'const' keyword.

skills.name: Variables
skills.desc: Variables in Go are used to store and manage data that can change during program execution. They are declared using the 'var' keyword.

skills.name: Strings
skills.desc: Strings are sequences of characters and are used to represent text in Go. They are immutable and can be manipulated using various built-in functions.

skills.name: Arrays
skills.desc: Arrays are fixed-size collections of elements with the same data type. The size of an array is specified at declaration and cannot be changed.

skills.name: Slices
skills.desc: Slices are dynamic, variable-length sequences built on top of arrays. They are more flexible and commonly used than arrays in Go.

skills.name: Maps
skills.desc: Maps are key-value pairs data structures in Go, also known as dictionaries or associative arrays in other languages.

skills.name: Structs
skills.desc: Structs are composite data types that group together variables of different data types. They are often used to define custom data structures.

skills.name: Pointers
skills.desc: Pointers are variables that store memory addresses of other variables. They are used to work with data indirectly and can be crucial for efficiency.

skills.name: For
skills.desc: 'For' loops are used for repetitive tasks and iterating over collections or ranges in Go.

skills.name: If Else
skills.desc: Conditional statements like 'if' and 'else' are used to make decisions and execute different code blocks based on conditions.

skills.name: Switch
skills.desc: 'Switch' statements are used for multi-way branching, allowing different code paths based on the value of an expression.

skills.name: Functions
skills.desc: Functions are blocks of reusable code that perform specific tasks and can accept parameters and return values.

skills.name: Range
skills.desc: 'Range' is used for iterating over data structures like arrays, slices, maps, and channels, providing a convenient way to access elements.

skills.name: Closures
skills.desc: Closures are anonymous functions that capture and retain the variables from their containing function's scope.

skills.name: Methods
skills.desc: Methods are functions associated with structs or types, enabling behavior and functionality for specific data structures.

skills.name: Interfaces
skills.desc: Interfaces define sets of methods that types can implement. They allow polymorphism and decoupling in Go programs.

skills.name: Struct Embedding
skills.desc: Struct embedding is a technique that allows a struct to inherit fields and methods from other structs, promoting code reuse.

skills.name: Errors
skills.desc: Errors in Go are values representing exceptional conditions that can be returned from functions to indicate failure.

skills.name: Panic
skills.desc: Panic is a built-in function that stops the normal execution of a Go program and triggers a stack unwinding process.

skills.name: Goroutines
skills.desc: Goroutines are lightweight threads of execution in Go, allowing concurrent execution of code.

skills.name: Channels
skills.desc: Channels are used for communication and synchronization between goroutines, facilitating safe concurrent programming.

skills.name: Select
skills.desc: 'Select' is used for multiplexing and handling multiple communication operations on channels.

skills.name: Waitgroups
skills.desc: Waitgroups are used to wait for a collection of goroutines to finish their execution before proceeding.

skills.name: Mutexes
skills.desc: Mutexes (short for mutual exclusion) are synchronization primitives used to protect critical sections of code from concurrent access.

skills.name: Reading Files
skills.desc: Reading files involves opening a file, reading its content, and processing the data within the file.

skills.name: Writing Files
skills.desc: Writing files involves creating, opening, and writing data to a file, allowing the storage of information persistently.

skills.name: File Paths
skills.desc: Working with file paths includes manipulating and constructing paths for file and directory operations.

skills.name: Directories
skills.desc: Directories in Go are used for organizing and managing collections of files. Operations include listing, creating, and removing directories.

skills.name: Temporary Files and Directories
skills.desc: Temporary files and directories are used for storing data temporarily and are automatically cleaned up when no longer needed.

skills.name: Sorting
skills.desc: Sorting involves arranging data in a specific order, and Go provides sorting functions for various data types.

skills.name: Text Templates
skills.desc: Text templates are used for generating structured text output, often for generating HTML or other text-based formats.

skills.name: Regular Expressions
skills.desc: Regular expressions enable pattern matching and text manipulation, providing powerful tools for string manipulation.

skills.name: JSON
skills.desc: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a widely used data interchange format, and Go provides libraries for encoding and decoding JSON data.

skills.name: Time
skills.desc: Working with time and dates, including operations like parsing, formatting, and time zone handling.

skills.name: Time Formatting Parsing
skills.desc: Formatting and parsing time values is essential for displaying and working with timestamps in various formats.

skills.name: Number Parsing
skills.desc: Number parsing involves converting strings to numerical data types, which is common in input validation and data processing.

skills.name: URL Parsing
skills.desc: URL parsing is used for extracting and manipulating components of URLs, making it easier to work with web-related tasks.

skills.name: base64 Encoding
skills.desc: base64 encoding is used for converting binary data into a text format, commonly used for encoding binary data in text-based formats like JSON.

skills.name: Testing and Benchmarking
skills.desc: Testing and benchmarking are crucial for ensuring the correctness and performance of Go code under various conditions.

skills.name: Command Line
skills.desc: Command line programming involves interacting with the command line interface (CLI) and parsing command-line arguments in Go applications.

skills.name: Environment Variables
skills.desc: Environment variables are used to configure and control the behavior of Go applications based on the surrounding environment.

skills.name: HTTP Client
skills.desc: Using the HTTP client in Go to make HTTP requests and interact with remote servers and APIs.

skills.name: Processes
skills.desc: Managing external processes and executing system commands from a Go program.

skills.name: Exit
skills.desc: Exiting Go programs gracefully and handling program termination with control over exit status.
